title: coleus monostachyus subsp. marrubiifolius
intro: Coleus monostachyus subsp. marrubiifolius, also known as Marrubium coleus, is a subspecies of the Coleus genus, a type of flowering plant native to tropical Africa. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues, fever, and inflammation due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Marrubium coleus include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and alleviating symptoms of fever, diarrhea, and dysentery. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and decoctions made from its leaves, which can be used to treat various health issues, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
